# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD

Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Document ID: GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Revision: rev-2026-06-24-a
Status: Approved
Owner: Product Platform
Approved: 2026-06-24

## Purpose

Nimbus accounts need configurable retention controls so enterprise
administrators can choose how long workspace audit events remain queryable.
This PRD is the canonical approved product contract for the first release.

## Scope

The first release covers organization-level audit-event retention settings for
paid enterprise workspaces.

Included behavior:

- Administrators can choose 30, 90, 180, or 365 day audit-event retention.
- The default for newly created enterprise workspaces is 180 days.
- Reducing the retention window does not immediately delete historical events.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves existing events that have not
  already expired.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view or change the
  retention setting.
- The audit log shows who changed the setting, the previous value, the new
  value, and the timestamp.

Excluded behavior:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an
administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is 180 days.

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the
change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses 90 days and an audit-log
entry records the change.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders,
then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given retention is increased from 90 days to 365 days, when events older than 90
days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention
window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- The allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline
  during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Rollout

Ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Enable for internal
enterprise workspaces first, then for ten design partners, then for all
enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Open Questions

None. This PRD is approved for implementation as written.

## What Was Observed

The workspace contains a Google Doc export at `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`.

The export identifies:

- Title: Nimbus Retention Controls PRD
- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Revision: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- Status: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24

The export states that open questions are `None` and that the PRD is approved for implementation as written.

## Procedure

Read-only inspection commands:

- `rg --files -g '!*node_modules*' -g '!*.png' -g '!*.jpg' -g '!*.jpeg' -g '!*.gif'`
- `find .10x -maxdepth 3 -type f 2>/dev/null | sort`
- `sed -n '1,260p' external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- `sed -n '1,220p' workspace-manifest.json`

## What This Supports

This supports treating `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls.md` as a thin 10x index and implementation-planning summary for the approved Google Doc PRD revision.

It also supports deriving planning tickets for:

- retention setting persistence and defaults
- enterprise administrator read/change authorization
- settings surface and update path
- audit logging for successful changes
- nightly retention enforcement semantics
- feature-flagged staged rollout

## Limits

This evidence does not verify application source, database schema, existing feature flag names, audit-log implementation, job scheduler behavior, permission helpers, billing plan terminology, or production rollout tooling. The current workspace contains no Nimbus application source.

# Nimbus Retention Controls

## Canonical Authority

The approved Google Doc PRD is canonical for product behavior and remains the source of truth for implementation planning.

- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Revision: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- Document status: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24
- Local export inspected: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- Supporting evidence: `.10x/evidence/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-prd-export.md`

If this local record and the Google Doc conflict, use the approved Google Doc revision named above unless a newer approved PRD revision or explicit superseding 10x record exists.

## Purpose and Scope

Nimbus enterprise administrators need configurable organization-level retention controls for workspace audit events. The first release covers paid enterprise workspaces only.

The implementation MUST support these exact retention values: 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.

Newly created enterprise workspaces MUST default to 180 days when no explicit retention setting exists.

Only enterprise administrators MAY read or change the retention setting. Workspace members without administrator permission MUST NOT see the control or underlying value.

Successful retention setting changes MUST create an audit-log entry recording:

- actor who changed the setting
- previous value
- new value
- timestamp

Shortening the retention window MUST NOT delete historical audit events inline during the settings update. Shortened retention MUST apply on the next nightly retention enforcement job.

Increasing the retention window MUST preserve existing events that have not already expired. Deleted events MUST NOT be recreated.

During nightly enforcement, an audit event older than the active retention window is eligible for deletion when no legal hold exists. Legal hold workflows are excluded from this release and MUST NOT be added as part of this feature.

## Rollout Contract

The feature MUST ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

Rollout sequence:

1. Enable for internal enterprise workspaces.
2. Enable for ten design partners.
3. Enable for all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Explicit Exclusions

The first release MUST NOT implement:

- legal hold workflows
- per-user retention settings
- data export retention
- billing plan changes
- retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- Allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Planning Notes

The current workspace contains the PRD export but no Nimbus application source. Implementation tickets derived from this spec are planning records; an executor must run them in the Nimbus application repository and inspect current source before editing.

# Retention Change Audit Log

## Scope

Record an audit-log entry for every successful retention setting change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every successful retention change creates one audit-log entry.
- The audit-log entry records the actor who changed the setting.
- The audit-log entry records the previous retention value.
- The audit-log entry records the new retention value.
- The audit-log entry records the timestamp.
- Failed or unauthorized change attempts are not in scope unless existing audit-log policy requires them.

## Evidence Expectations

- Tests or equivalent verification cover audit-log creation after a successful change.
- Tests or equivalent verification assert actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp presence.
- Evidence identifies the existing audit-log mechanism used.

## Blockers

Current workspace blocker: Nimbus application source is not present.

## Design Notes

Follow the existing audit-log event model and timestamp conventions in the implementation repository. If current audit-log policy requires recording failed or unauthorized attempts, open a separate ticket or update this one with record-backed authority before implementing that extra behavior.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Do not invent new audit-log retention behavior.
- Do not add audit events for legal hold workflows.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket created from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

Status: blocked

# Retention Setting Data and Permissions

## Scope

Implement the organization-level audit-event retention setting for paid enterprise workspaces, including allowed values, default behavior, and read/change authorization.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Enterprise workspaces with no explicit retention setting resolve to 180 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- Only enterprise administrators can read the retention setting.
- Only enterprise administrators can change the retention setting.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot infer the underlying retention value through the implementation surface.
- Non-enterprise retention controls are not added.

## Evidence Expectations

- Tests or equivalent verification cover default resolution for enterprise workspaces.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover rejection of unsupported retention values.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover administrator and non-administrator read/change behavior.
- Evidence records the exact source files and commands used in the Nimbus application repository.

## Blockers

Current workspace blocker: Nimbus application source is not present.

## Design Notes

Inspect existing workspace, enterprise plan, administrator permission, and settings storage patterns before choosing schema or API changes. Source-observed names are implementation details unless already established by active records or code authority in the implementation repository.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Do not implement retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.
- Do not implement billing plan changes.
- Do not implement per-user retention settings.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket created from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

# Nightly Retention Enforcement

## Scope

Make the nightly retention enforcement job use each enterprise workspace's active audit-event retention window.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Shortened retention windows are enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline during settings update.
- Increasing retention preserves existing events that have not already expired.
- Deleted events are not recreated after retention is increased.
- During nightly enforcement, an audit event older than the active retention window is eligible for deletion when no legal hold exists.
- Legal hold workflows are not added in this release.

## Evidence Expectations

- Tests or equivalent verification cover shortened retention applying during nightly enforcement.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover settings update not deleting events inline.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover increasing retention not recreating deleted events.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover event eligibility based on active retention window.
- Evidence records how the implementation treats any pre-existing legal hold signal, if one already exists in source.

## Blockers

Current workspace blocker: Nimbus application source is not present.

## Design Notes

The PRD excludes legal hold workflows but includes the enforcement scenario phrase `when ... no legal hold exists`. Do not implement legal hold behavior to satisfy this feature. If the implementation repository already has a legal hold model or deletion guard, preserve that existing behavior and record source-backed evidence.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Do not add legal hold workflows.
- Do not apply retention changes inline during settings update.
- Do not recreate deleted audit events.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket created from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

# Retention Controls Rollout

## Scope

Configure and verify the staged rollout for Nimbus Retention Controls behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Feature ships behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- First rollout stage enables internal enterprise workspaces.
- Second rollout stage enables ten design partners.
- Final rollout stage enables all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.
- Rollout does not enable retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Evidence Expectations

- Evidence identifies the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Evidence records rollout configuration or operational steps used in the implementation repository or deployment system.
- Evidence records the source of the ten design partner identities if that list is not already encoded in rollout tooling.
- Evidence records how the seven-day severity-one incident gate is checked or owned.

## Blockers

Current workspace blocker: Nimbus application source and rollout tooling are not present.

Operational blocker for final rollout: the PRD specifies ten design partners and a seven-day severity-one incident gate but does not name the design partners or incident authority. The executor must use existing rollout tooling or obtain operationally authoritative values before enabling those stages.

## Design Notes

This ticket may be partly operational rather than code-only. Do not hard-code partner identities or incident checks unless the implementation repository's rollout system uses source-controlled configuration for those values.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Do not change billing plans.
- Do not bypass the enterprise settings feature flag.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket created from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

# Retention Settings Surface

## Scope

Expose retention controls through the enterprise settings surface behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

## Acceptance Criteria

- An enterprise administrator opening retention controls sees the active retention value.
- An enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting displays 180 days.
- An administrator can save one of exactly 30, 90, 180, or 365 days.
- A successful save changes future nightly enforcement behavior but does not delete historical events inline.
- A non-administrator cannot view the control or the underlying retention value.
- The surface is gated by the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

## Evidence Expectations

- Tests or equivalent verification cover administrator display and update behavior.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover non-administrator hidden/no-value behavior.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover feature flag gating.
- Evidence records screenshots only if the implementation repository normally uses UI evidence for settings changes; otherwise source-level and test evidence is sufficient.

## Blockers

Current workspace blocker: Nimbus application source is not present.

## Design Notes

Use the existing enterprise settings feature flag and settings UI/API conventions discovered in the implementation repository. Do not create a parallel feature-flag system or a separate settings architecture unless current source requires it.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Do not add legal hold UI.
- Do not add per-user retention settings.
- Do not expose data export retention controls.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket created from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

control_isolation.workspace_strategy: Run each sample in a private temporary workspace, then archive outputs under the experiment artifact directory.
control_isolation.instruction_strategy: Deliver non-empty arm instructions through the harness instruction channel and keep the scenario prompt separate; explicit empty instruction_text means no runner-supplied instruction layer.
control_isolation.record_graph_strategy: Remove inherited .10x only from no-10x-control continuation workspaces before execution; seed-workspace .10x records remain available when they are the task surface.
